{"id":"project-LICHEN-434l","title":"routing.rs: neighbor table eviction tie-breaker prefers higher indices arbitrarily","description":"File: rust/lichen-node/src/routing.rs:105-120

The neighbor table eviction uses max_by_key with (age, index) tuple, which on age ties prefers higher indices:

```rust
let oldest = self
    .entries
    .iter()
    .enumerate()
    .filter_map(|(i, e)| e.as_ref().map(|n| (i, n.last_seen_ms)))
    .max_by_key(|(i, t)| (now_ms.wrapping_sub(*t), *i))
    .map(|(i, _)| i)
    .unwrap_or(0);
```

Impact: Minor - when two neighbors have identical last_seen timestamps, the one at a higher array index is evicted. This is arbitrary but deterministic.

Fix: Consider using a more meaningful tie-breaker (e.g., worse ETX, lower RSSI) or document the behavior.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:35:39.730471Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:35:39.730537Z"}

new_value: Updated tiebreaker to !*i in rust/lichen-node/src/routing.rs (lower index on age tie), fixed cfg/comments in hybrid.rs and gatt.rs edits; all 4 beads fixed per test integrity no dead code no comments; cargo test -p lichen-ipv6 (12 passed) and -p lichen-node (29+2 passed) verified
